source_files.notes: Read from caller-approved manifest in provided order.

topics: Short source brief to publishable summary
Lifecycle stages, deliverables, checkpoints, and constraints
key_points: The workflow objective is to turn a short source brief into a publishable summary.
The lifecycle is explicitly split into four stages: Intake, Drafting, Review, and Finalization.
Each stage has a named deliverable: `project-brief`, `draft-summary`, `review-notes`, and `final-summary`.
After Review, a reviewer must either approve the draft or request one revision.
Revision is limited to one round.
Intake and Drafting must not be merged.
The final output must preserve the original topic and audience.

phases.name: Intake
phases.purpose: Read the source brief and extract core framing information.
risks: If topic, audience, or constraints are extracted incorrectly, downstream drafting may drift from source intent.

phases.name: Drafting
phases.purpose: Use the project brief to draft the summary.
risks: Draft quality depends on completeness and accuracy of the project brief.

phases.name: Review
phases.purpose: Check the draft for clarity, completeness, and tone, and determine whether revision is needed.
approvals: Reviewer must either approve the draft or request one revision.
risks: Only one revision round is allowed, so unresolved issues may need careful prioritization.

phases.name: Finalization
phases.purpose: Apply approved review changes and produce the finished output.
risks: Final output must preserve the original topic and audience.

checkpoints.name: Post-Review decision
checkpoints.notes: After Review, a reviewer must either approve the draft or request one revision.

suggested_role_boundaries.name: Intake/Drafting author
suggested_role_boundaries.why: The source links Drafting directly to the `project-brief` produced during Intake, suggesting a natural handoff or shared ownership boundary.

suggested_role_boundaries.name: Reviewer
suggested_role_boundaries.why: The source explicitly names a reviewer decision point after Review.

suggested_role_boundaries.name: Finalizer
suggested_role_boundaries.why: Finalization applies approved review changes, which may be handled as a distinct finishing step.

non_merge_constraints.reason: The source explicitly states: "Do not merge Intake and Drafting."

terminology.definition: The Intake deliverable containing the extracted topic, audience, and key constraints.
The Drafting deliverable produced from the project brief.
The Review deliverable documenting clarity, completeness, tone findings, and revision direction if needed.
The Finalization deliverable after approved review changes are applied.

structure.purpose: States the workflow goal: turning a short source brief into a publishable summary.
Defines the lifecycle phases, activities, and deliverables.
Defines the approval/revision decision after Review.
Defines simplicity, revision budget, output preservation requirements, and non-merge semantics.

citations.quote: Create a very small workflow that turns a short source brief into a publishable summary.
Extract the topic, audience, and key constraints.
If issues are found, request one revision round.
citations.reason: Defines the revision loop and budget.

citations.quote: After Review, a reviewer must either approve the draft or request one revision.
citations.reason: Defines the explicit checkpoint and approval decision.

citations.quote: Do not merge Intake and Drafting.
The final output must preserve the original topic and audience.
